ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT SISTERS OF THE PRESENTATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT (LEAVE TO AMEND)
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-06-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SISTERS OF THE PRESENTATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, FOURTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION TO GIVE THE PLAINTIFF AN OPPORTUNITY, IF SHE IS ABLE TO DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE COGNIZABLE CLAIMS FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE 1102.5 AND WRONGFUL DISCHARGE IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT ALL FOUR OF HER FEHA CLAIMS LACK MERIT AS A MATTER OF LAW. THE THIRD, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT, ARE BASED ON ASSERTED VIOLATIONS OF FEHA, WHICH IS NOT PERMISSIBLE AGAINST THE DEMURRING PARTY. HOWEVER, IT MAY BE POSSIBLE THAT PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE ONE OR MORE OF THESE CLAIMS NOT BASED ON ASSERTED VIOLATIONS OF FEHA. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SISTERS OF THE PRESENTATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 28, 2016 AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/EG)
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-15-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR DEFENDANT SISTERS OF THE PRESENTATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT HEARING SET FOR JAN-28-2016 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SISTERS OF THE PRESENTATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE LABOR CODE SECTION 98.6 DOES NOT APPLY TO RELIGIOUS ASSOCIATIONS OR CORPORATIONS SUCH AS DEFENDANT SISTERS OF THE PRESENTATION. (SEE LABOR CODE SECTION 98.6(D).) THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO AMEND THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE COGNIZABLE CLAIMS FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE 1102.5 AND WRONGFUL DISCHARGE IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES FACTS WHICH SUPPORT PLAINTIFF'S SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE 1102.5 AND PLAINTIFF'S THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL DISCHARGE IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. (FAC PARAGRAPHS 13-43; 49-58.) THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT A SHAM PLEADING. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE FOURTH, FIFTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT DID NOT GRANT PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO ADD NEW CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF DID NOT NOTIFY THE COURT IN HER OPPOSITION TO THE ORIGINAL DEMURRER THAT SHE SOUGHT TO ADD THE ADDITIONAL CAUSES OF ACTION, AND PLAINTIFF DID NOT FILE HER NEW CAUSES OF ACTION IN ACCORDANCE WITH CCP SECTION 472. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-28-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT SISTERS OF THE PRESENTATIONS'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SISTERS OF THE PRESENTATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION IS GRANTED. STP PRESENTED SUFFICIENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE TO SATISFY ITS BURDEN THAT THERE WAS A LEGITIMATE NON-RETALIATORY REASON FOR THE TERMINATION OF THE EMPLOYMENT OF PLAINTIFF ROBIN LEE-DANDRIDGE AND MS. LEE-DANDRIDGE FAILED TO PRESENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE OR PRETEXT OR RETALIATORY ANIMUS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

APPEARANCES - ATTY. SHAUNA N. CORREIA OF WEINTRAUB/TOBIN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-772-9655 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER,

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Sisters of the Presentation's motion for summary judgment motion is granted. STP presented sufficient admissible evidence to satisfy its burden that there was a legitimate non-retaliatory reason for the termination of the employment of plaintiff Robin Lee-Dandridge and Ms. Lee-Dandridge failed to present admissible evidence or pretext or retaliatory animus. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Mar-09-2016 Off Calendar

Defendant Sisters Of The Presentation'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Sisters Of The Presentation's Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to first cause of action is sustained without leave to amend because Labor Code section 98.6 does not apply to religious associations or corporations such as Defendant Sisters of the Presentation. (See Labor Code section 98.6(d).) The demurrer to the second and third causes of action is overruled. The court granted Plaintiff leave to amend the original complaint to allege cognizable claims for intentional infliction of emotional distress, violation of Labor Code 1102.5 and wrongful discharge in violation of public policy. The first amended complaint alleges facts which support Plaintiff's second cause of action for violation of Labor Code 1102.5 and Plaintiff's third cause of action for wrongful discharge in violation of public policy. (FAC paragraphs 13-43; 49-58.) The first amended complaint is not a sham pleading. Demurrer is sustained as to the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action. The court did not grant plaintiff leave to add new causes of action, Plaintiff did not notify the court in her opposition to the original demurrer that she sought to add the additional causes of action, and Plaintiff did not file her new causes of action in accordance with CCP section 472. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Continued To Jan-27-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SISTERS OF THE PRESENTATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the first, second, fourth and seventh causes of action and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the third, fifth and sixth causes of action to give the plaintiff an opportunity, if she is able to do so in good faith, to allege cognizable claims for intentional infliction of emotional distress, violation of Labor Code 1102.5 and wrongful discharge in violation of public policy. Plaintiff concedes that all four of her FEHA claims lack merit as a matter of law. The third, fifth and sixth causes of action, as alleged in the complaint, are based on asserted violations of FEHA, which is not permissible against the demurring party. However, it may be possible that plaintiff can allege one or more of these claims not based on asserted violations of FEHA. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
